rulings.Case Title: LSC 906-908 BROADWAY LLC VS. STARTUP TEMPLE HOLDINGS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Judgment To Set The Deficiency Judgment Against Guarantor Pavel Cherkashin, Only; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Eugene Rozovsky In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 7, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF LSC 906-908 BROADWAY LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO AMEND JUDGMENT TO SET THE DEFICIENCY JUDGMENT AGAINST GUARANTOR PAVEL CHERKASHIN, ONLY; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES AND DECLARATION OF EUGENE ROZOVSKY IN SUPPORT THEREOF is GRANTED in the amount of $4,031,324.96 representing the outstanding principal amount and interest prior to the sale on March 2, 2023 ($2,087.18/day) and after the sale until October 26, 2023 ($1,096.73/day). The creditor bid was accepted on March 2, 2023. Per May 11, 2023 stipulation and order the ownership transferred as of that date to the Plaintiff upon the acceptance of the bid. No other amounts claimed in this motion are supported by authority (none cited in the Points and Authorities) or admissible evidence (see Rozovsky declaration and exhibits thereto). The Court notes the discrepancy in the amounts sought in a prior motion versus the amounts sought in the current motion on various items without any explanation. (e.g. Rozovky July 21, 2023 declaration regarding $35,574.53 insurance premiums advanced between May 2022 and the date of the declaration; and Rozovky November 7, 2023 declaration regarding $10,305.43 in insurance premiums advanced between May 2022 and the date of the declaration). The Court further notes the absence of a declaration of counsel regarding the reasonableness of attorney's fees and the necessity of work performed on the case in addition to the lack of any authority regarding entitlement to the fees (See Points and Authorities in support of the motion). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NAIFEH AZAR VS. MARIYA LYUBLINA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Order Granting Lyublina'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions Entered On Date Of Nov. 2, 2023 Pursuant To Ccp 473(D), Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, Declaration Of Naifeh Azar
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 7, 2024 line 2. PLAINTIFF NAIFEH AZAR NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE AND VACATE ORDER GRANTING LYUBLINA'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS ENTERED ON DATE OF NOV. 2, 2023 PURSUANT TO CCP 473(D), MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION OF NAIFEH AZAR is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The motion to vacate the order is granted for the limited purpose of Defendant preparing an order reflecting October 24, 2023 ruling with an appropriate title reflecting the substance of the ruling. Specifically, no later than 9:00 a.m. on February 7, 2024 Defendant shall deliver to department 501 a hard copy of an Amended order reflecting the October 24, 2023 ruling verbatim. The order shall be titled "Amended Order Re: Defendant's Motion for Terminating and Monetary Sanctions." Motion is otherwise denied. Parties were provided detailed instructions and disclosures regarding hearing handled by judges pro tem in the tentative rulings issued for October 10, 2023 and October 24, 2023. The same instructions and disclosures are issued for every discovery hearing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH G. ROMERO VS. ALBERTO TOLEDO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 7, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT ALBERTO TOLEDO, JUANA TOLDEDO, ABRIL TOLEDO, JOSE PERDOMO, XIOMARA DOMINGUEZ MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts showing that "recovery of possession" and "eviction" are interchangeable within the meaning of Regulation 12.14(b)(8)(iv). rulings.Case Title: MARY PHAM VS. SVETLANA KONOVICH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 7, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT SVETLANA KONOVICH DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. This unlawful detainer action is based on two notices served on October 4, 2023 with an expiration date of October 10, 2023. The first notice demanded that rent for the months of April to September 2023 be paid within 3 days. The second Notice terminated the tenancy based on nuisance on July 31, 2023. Two causes of action for unlawful detainer are not alleged in the alternative. Plaintiff shall have 5 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint reconciling the demand for rent on October 4, 2023, months after the alleged nuisance, with the termination of tenancy based on that nuisance. Specially, the Plaintiff shall allege whether Plaintiff intended to accept the rent, if tendered, pursuant to the Notice to Pay Rent or Quit and re-instate the tenancy. If Plaintiff had no such intent, Plaintiff must allege facts giving rise to the unlawful detainer based on failure to comply with 3 Day Notice to Pay or Quit consistent with Plaintiff's lack of intent to accept the rent as demanded. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
